# i18n

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `i18n`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| ------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `i18n.glibcLocales` | `absolute path` | Customized glibcLocales package providing the LOCALE_ARCHIVE\_\* environment variable. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable an additional input method type. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.enabled` | `null or one of “fcitx”, “fcitx5”, “nabi”, “uim”, “hime”, “kime”` | Deprecated - use type and enable = true instead |
| `i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.addons` | `list of package` | Enabled Fcitx5 addons. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.fcitx5-with-addons` | `package` | The fcitx5 package to use. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.ignoreUserConfig` | `boolean` | Ignore the user configures. Warning : When this is enabled, the user config files are totally ignored and the user dict can’t be saved and loaded. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.quickPhrase` | `attribute set of string` | Quick phrases. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.quickPhraseFiles` | `attribute set of absolute path` | Quick phrase files. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.settings.addons` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | The addon configures in conf folder in ini format with global sections. Each item is written to the corresponding file. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.settings.globalOptions` | `open submodule of attribute set of section of an INI file (attrs of INI atom (null, bool, int, float or string))` | The global options in config file in ini format. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.settings.inputMethod` | `open submodule of attribute set of section of an INI file (attrs of INI atom (null, bool, int, float or string))` | The input method configure in profile file in ini format. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.themes` | `lazy attribute set of (submodule)` | Themes to be written to $XDG_DATA_HOME/fcitx5/themes/${name} |
| `i18n.inputMethod.fcitx5.waylandFrontend` | `boolean` | Use the Wayland input method frontend. See Using Fcitx 5 on Wayland . |
| `i18n.inputMethod.kime.config` | | |
| `i18n.inputMethod.kime.extraConfig` | `strings concatenated with “\n”` | kime configuration. Refer to https://github.com/Riey/kime/blob/develop/docs/CONFIGURATION.md for details on supported values. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.package` | | |
| `i18n.inputMethod.type` | `null or one of “fcitx”, “fcitx5”, “nabi”, “uim”, “hime”, “kime”` | Select the enabled input method. Input methods are software to input symbols that are not available on standard input devices. |
| `i18n.inputMethod.uim.toolbar` | `one of “gtk”, “gtk3”, “gtk-systray”, “gtk3-systray”, “qt4”` | Selected UIM toolbar. |
